allow:
Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Initial deployment of Ms. Luminara''s Quiz Lab

Features:
- Gamified A&P quiz with 189+ questions across 7 topics
- D20 RPG system with stats, rolls, and equipment
- XP/leveling with streak multipliers and Lucky Strike bonus
- Multiple study modes: Standard, Jeopardy, Blitz, Lightning, Spaced Review
- PWA support for iPhone "Add to Home Screen"
- Offline functionality via service worker
- Isotope-tagged semantic memory system
- Zone of Proximal Development scaffolding
- Spaced repetition for optimal retention

Topics covered:
- Brain structure and function
- Nervous system and reflexes
- Anatomical foundations
- Tissue types
- Autonomic nervous system
- Special senses
- Endocrine system

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Reduce repetitive phrases in quiz explanations

Cleaned up overused phrases across all question banks while
maintaining Ms. Luminara''s distinctive teaching voice:

- Removed excessive "Come closer/with me" openings
- Removed formulaic "Isn't that beautiful/exquisite?"" endings
- Removed repetitive ""Here's where it gets delicious/beautiful"
- Removed "Let yourself sink into this mechanism" filler
- Removed "Stay with me" and "Wait for it" interruptions
- Removed "The body does this thousands of times per second"

The explanations retain their pedagogical structure, physics-first
metaphors, and warmth - just without the repetitive scaffolding
that made them predictable. Ms. Luminara''s voice is preserved
through varied phrasing.

Files modified: 31 question bank JSON files across all 7 topics

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add roguelike Gauntlet mode with boss battles and arcade mechanics

The Luminara Gauntlet - a new roguelike adventure mode featuring:

Boss System (000.15-boss-system.js):
- 6 themed bosses: The Forgetful One, The Procrastinator, The Anxiety
  Spiral, The Distraction Demon, The Imposter, Ms. Luminara''s Shadow
- Each boss has unique HP, armor, abilities, phases, and weaknesses
- BossEncounter class for battle state management
- Secret boss unlocked after defeating all regular bosses

Run Manager (000.16-run-manager.js):
- Wave-based progression with boss every 2 waves
- 4 difficulty modes: Study Session, Standard, Challenge, Nightmare
- Score calculation with streak, boss, time, and HP bonuses
- Arcade tokens earned from runs for power-up purchases
- Run history and statistics tracking

Power-ups (000.17-powerups.js):
- 15+ silly power-ups like Caffeine Rush, Rubber Duck Debugging,
  Call Mom, Wikipedia Rabbit Hole, Grade Curve
- Unlock system based on gameplay milestones
- Active effects: damage multipliers, shields, hints, healing
- Arcade token shop integration

High Scores (000.18-high-scores.js):
- Top 10 leaderboard with rank titles
- 12 rank titles from "Study Sprout" to "Legendary Scholar"
- 20+ achievements for various accomplishments
- Fun anatomy facts shown after each run
- Personal bests and career stats tracking

D20 Combat Extensions (000.7-d20-system.js):
- attackBoss(): INT-based attack roll vs boss armor
- defendAgainstBoss(): CON-based damage reduction
- specialAbilityCheck(): WIS-based special abilities

Boss Loot Tables (000.8-loot-system.js):
- Each boss has unique guaranteed drops and special items
- Consumables with temporary stat boosts
- Increased gem and gold drops from bosses

UI Additions:
- New Gauntlet tab in mode navigation
- Boss arena with HP bars, taunts, abilities, and actions
- Attack/Special/Defend battle actions
- Victory and defeat screens with score breakdown
- High scores leaderboard overlay
- Power-up shop with unlock progress
- Run progress bar during active runs

CSS Styling:
- Boss arena with animated glow effects
- Difficulty selection grid
- Battle action buttons with hover states
- Score breakdown and loot displays
- Power-up activation animations
- Responsive mobile layout

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Fix syntax errors in loot-system and renderer classes

Fixed class structure issues causing JavaScript parse errors:

000.8-loot-system.js:
- Removed premature class-closing brace before boss loot methods
- Converted `static BOSS_LOOT_TABLES` to `getBossLootTable(bossId)` 
  method for browser compatibility (ES6 static class fields not
  universally supported)

000.2-renderer.js:
- Removed premature class-closing brace before boss UI methods
  (renderBossEncounter, showBossDamage, etc.)

Both files now parse correctly and LootSystem initializes properly.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Switch to network-first caching strategy

Truly roguelike - every refresh fetches fresh files from the server.
Cache is now only used as offline fallback.

Changes:
- Network-first fetch strategy (was cache-first)
- Cache updated with fresh content after each fetch
- Offline mode still works via cached fallback
- Bumped to v4 to force immediate takeover

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add mobile-optimized version for iOS/touch devices

- mobile.html - Streamlined mobile layout with bottom tab navigation

- Bottom navigation bar (iOS tab bar style)
- Larger touch targets (54px minimum)
- Simplified mode tabs (icons only)

Access via: luminara.natalie-eiryk.com/mobile.html

Add prominent RPG character bar to mobile version

Character Bar (sticky top HUD):

Quick Stats Row:
- INT/WIS/CON modifiers (tap for full character sheet)

making the loot hunt and character progression the star of the show.

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Fix mobile script loading order - add missing scaffold-remediation

Scripts must load in dependency order:
1. persistence.js (base storage)
2. gamification.js (depends on persistence) 
3. scaffolding.js
4. d20-system.js
5. loot-system.js
6. scaffold-remediation.js (was missing!)
7. boss/run/powerups/highscores
8. renderer.js (depends on all systems)
9. app.js (initializes everything - must be last)

Also bumped SW cache to v6 and added scaffold-remediation to cache list.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Restructure scaffolds into subfolders (Dewey-compatible)

Major restructuring for token efficiency and organization:

File structure change:
- Scaffolds (prereqs) extracted from main question files
- Each topic now has a *-scaffolds/ subfolder
- Individual scaffold files: {questionId}-scaffolds.json
- Main question files now reference scaffoldFile instead of embedding prereqs

Example new structure:
  200-nerves/
    200.5-cranial-nerves.json (main questions, lean)
    200.5-scaffolds/
      200.5.01-scaffolds.json
      200.5.02-scaffolds.json
      ...

Code changes (000.1-app.js):
- Added loadScaffoldsForQuestion() for lazy loading
- Modified loadCategory() to tag questions with _categoryFolder
- Modified startStudyBank() and startStudy() to preload scaffolds
- Scaffolds cached on question object after first load

Benefits:
- Main question files ~35% smaller
- Scaffolds load only when needed
- Easy to add more scaffold questions per topic
- Clean Dewey decimal organization
- Token-efficient for LLM context

211 scaffold files extracted across 17 question banks.
Bumped service worker to v7.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add foundation-level scaffolds for CN I, II, III (first 3 cranial nerves)

New scaffolding approach with difficulty levels:
- "foundation": Simple, Ms. Frizzle-style questions for beginners
- "intermediate": Original prereq questions for reinforcement

Each scaffold file now has ~9 questions:
- 7 foundation questions building basic concepts
- 2 intermediate questions bridging to main question

Foundation scaffolds cover:
- 200.5.01 (CN I Olfactory): What is smell, what does olfactory mean,
  what are cranial nerves, Roman numeral basics
- 200.5.02 (CN II Optic): What is vision, cable analogy for nerves,
  what does optic mean, what is blindness
- 200.5.03 (CN III Oculomotor): Eye movements, pupil basics,
  what does oculomotor mean, accommodation concept

This establishes the pattern for remaining 19 cranial nerve questions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git -C "G:/Studying_Help/ms_luminara_quiz" commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add foundation-level scaffolds for CN IV-XII (19 questions)

Completed foundation scaffolds for all 22 cranial nerve questions.
Each scaffold file now contains 9 questions:
- 7 foundation questions (Ms. Frizzle style, simple concepts)
- 2 intermediate questions (bridge to main question)

Questions covered:
- 200.5.04-200.5.12: CN IV-XII basic functions
- 200.5.13-200.5.15: Clinical examination patterns
- 200.5.16: Vagus nerve - hoarse voice and swallowing
- 200.5.17: CN III palsy - ptosis, dilated pupil, down and out
- 200.5.18: CN VII - taste, tears, facial movement
- 200.5.19: Parotid surgery - CN VII motor vs CN V sensory
- 200.5.20: Trigeminal neuralgia - stabbing facial pain
- 200.5.21: Recurrent laryngeal nerve after thyroid surgery
- 200.5.22: Trochlear nerve - double vision looking down

Foundation scaffolds build basic concepts progressively:
- What is the function (smell, vision, movement)?
- What does the nerve name mean?
- What are cranial nerves?
- Simple clinical correlations

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add foundation-level scaffolds for spinal nerves and plexuses (21 questions)

Expanded scaffold files from 2 questions each to 9 questions each
(7 foundation + 2 intermediate difficulty levels):

200.1-spinal (10 questions, 90 scaffolds):
- Dorsal/ventral rami anatomy
- Motor vs sensory root localization  
- Brachial plexus organization (roots, trunks, divisions, cords)
- Ganglia vs nuclei terminology
- Reflex arc components
- Brown-Sequard syndrome (cord hemisection)
- Syringomyelia (central cord lesion)
- Spinal shock progression (flaccid to spastic)
- Dorsal column syndrome (proprioception loss)
- Anterior spinal artery syndrome

200.3-plexuses (11 questions, 99 scaffolds):
- Ulnar nerve at elbow ("funny bone")
- Phrenic nerve and cervical plexus
- Brachial plexus organization
- Lumbar plexus (femoral/obturator nerves)
- Sacral plexus and sciatic nerve
- Erb-Duchenne palsy (C5-C6, waiter''s tip)
- Saturday night palsy (radial nerve compression)
- Radial nerve in spiral groove
- Carpal tunnel syndrome (median nerve)
- Common fibular nerve (foot drop)
- Klumpke''s palsy (C8-T1, claw hand)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add wrongExplains to all scaffolds in 100-brain and 200-nerves sections

Comprehensive scaffold improvements across 107 files:

100-brain section (20 files):
- 100.1-structure-scaffolds (16 files): Brain localization, regions,
  brainstem, cortex composition, basal nuclei, corpus callosum, white
  matter, corpora quadrigemina, pathways, Broca''s area, dermatomes,
  cerebellum, visual pathways, hippocampus/memory
- 100.2-meninges-csf-scaffolds (4 files): Blood-brain barrier, ependymal
  cells, subarachnoid space, normal CSF composition

200-nerves section (87 files):
- 200.1-spinal-scaffolds (10 files): Spinal cord anatomy and pathways
- 200.2-receptors-scaffolds (11 files): Sensory receptor types
- 200.3-plexuses-scaffolds (11 files): Nerve plexus anatomy
- 200.4-reflexes-scaffolds (10 files): Reflex arc components and testing
- 200.5-cranial-scaffolds (22 files): All 12 cranial nerves
- 200.6-autonomic-scaffolds (22 files): Sympathetic/parasympathetic

All scaffolds now include:
- wrongExplains field explaining why each wrong answer is incorrect
- Definitional "What is..." questions at start of each set
- 9 questions per scaffold (7 foundation, 2 intermediate)
- Key terms emphasized in CAPS for memory retention
- Converted True/False questions to 4-option multiple choice

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
